input: My train was delayed by more than 3 hours. Can I get a refund or compensation on Indian Railways?
reference_output: Yes. If your train is delayed by 3+ hours, file a TDR (Ticket Deposit Receipt) on irctc.co.in within 72 hours for e-tickets, or at the station for counter tickets. A full refund is processed after Railway verification (60–90 days). No additional monetary compensation is provided.

input: My train was cancelled by Indian Railways. How do I get my money back?
reference_output: For e-tickets, the full refund is automatically credited to your original payment source within 5–7 days — no TDR needed. For counter tickets, claim a refund at any PRS counter within 3 days of the scheduled departure. Tatkal tickets are also fully refunded in case of cancellation.

input: I want to cancel my confirmed train ticket. What charges will be deducted?
reference_output: Cancellation charges: more than 48 hrs before — flat fee (₹60–₹240 by class); 48–12 hrs before — 25% of fare; 12–4 hrs before — 50% of fare; less than 4 hrs or after departure — no refund. Tatkal tickets have stricter rules and higher charges.

input: My ticket is still on the waitlist. Will I get a refund if it doesn't get confirmed?
reference_output: Yes. Unconfirmed waitlisted e-tickets are auto-cancelled after chart preparation, and a full refund (minus a small service charge) is credited within 5–7 days. For counter tickets, cancel before chart preparation for a refund; after that, file a TDR.

input: I have an RAC ticket. What are my rights during the journey?
reference_output: An RAC ticket allows you to board the train and share a side-lower berth. If a confirmed passenger is absent, the TTE may upgrade you to a full berth. If you choose not to travel, cancel before chart preparation for a refund; otherwise file a TDR.

input: My bag was stolen during my train journey on Indian Railways. What should I do?
reference_output: Report the theft immediately to the TTE or RPF on the train and file an FIR at the nearest GRP (Railway Police) station. Call helpline 139 to report the incident. Indian Railways is liable only if the theft occurred due to negligence of railway staff.

input: I am a person with a disability. What concessions and facilities does Indian Railways offer?
reference_output: Divyangjan passengers get 25–75% fare concession depending on disability type and class, reserved lower berths, and wheelchair assistance at major stations. One escort also gets a concession. Book via IRCTC with a valid disability certificate and pre-book assistance through helpline 139.

input: The TTE on my train was rude and asked for a bribe. How do I complain?
reference_output: File a complaint on the RailMadad portal (railmadad.indianrailways.gov.in) or call helpline 139. For bribery, report to the Railway Vigilance or Central Vigilance Commission (CVC). Note the TTE's name, badge number, and train details as evidence.

input: What are the rules for booking and cancelling a Tatkal ticket on Indian Railways?
reference_output: Tatkal booking opens 1 day before journey (10 AM for AC, 11 AM for non-AC) with an added surcharge. Tatkal tickets are non-refundable on passenger cancellation. Refunds are only given if the train is cancelled, ticket remains waitlisted, or train is delayed 3+ hours and you choose not to travel.

input: What happens if I am caught travelling without a valid ticket on an Indian Railway train?
reference_output: Under Section 137 of the Railways Act, 1989, you must pay the full fare from the originating station plus a penalty of ₹250 or single journey fare (whichever is higher). The TTE collects this on the spot. Refusal to pay can result in being handed over to the Railway Police (GRP).
